# Build Packet ‚Äî 8d: Item / Product master (bidirectional onboarding ‚Üí ERPNext-mastered)

**This is an approved, frozen build packet. It is your input, not a working file. Do not edit this file or SPEC.md. If anything here is wrong, incomplete, or contradicts the committed code, STOP and report it ‚Äî do not silently reconcile or build around it (CLAUDE.md ¬ß0).**

## What this is, and a warning about its size

8d is the **largest** master ‚Äî a bidirectional item sync with a two-phase model (supervised bidirectional onboarding, then ERPNext-mastered steady state). Read ¬ß8.1 (appended in full) before anything else; it is the authoritative model and this packet implements it exactly.

**Build it in STAGES, in the order below, testing each stage green before starting the next.** Do NOT attempt the whole bidirectional engine in one shot ‚Äî it will be untestable. The stages are dependency-ordered: the pull is read-only toward EasyEcom and proves the substrate; the push mutates EasyEcom and must come after. Commit at the end of each stage (locally; the user reviews/pushes).

This packet depends on: the Field Mapping engine (¬ß5/¬ß8.0), 8c's tax resolver (`resolve_and_stamp_tax`), the 8a per-record savepoint helper (`_isolation.py`), and the workflow-fixture pattern (+ its 5 captured gotchas, incl. `safe_eval`/`len()`).

## The five EasyEcom Product Master API functions (all grounded in real payloads + the API doc)

- `GET /Products/GetProductMaster` ‚Äî the pull. Params: `includeLocations` (1 = account-wide, USE THIS), `updated_after` (incremental), `active`, `product_type`, `category`, `cpIds`, `limit` (max 200), `custom_fields`. Cursor pagination via `nextUrl`. Headers: `x-api-key` + `Bearer <jwt>`.
- `GET /Products/GetProductMastersCount` ‚Äî `{count: N}`; for progress/paging math.
- `POST /Products/CreateMasterProduct` ‚Äî push-create; returns `{data: {product_id}}`. Mandatory: Brand, Sku, Category, ModelNumber, Cost, Weight, Length, Height, Width, TaxRate, materialType. itemType (Child=0/Combo=1/Kit=2). subProducts (‚â•2, only itemType 1/2).
- `POST /Products/UpdateMasterProduct` ‚Äî push-update; keys on sku OR productId; partial updates OK.
- `POST /Products/ActivateDeactivateProduct` ‚Äî keys on product_id; status 1/0.

**Doc-vs-payload divergences already identified (handle the PAYLOAD reality, not the doc's):**
- `product_type` is a STRING in the response (`normal_product`/`variant_parent`/`child_product`/`combo_product`) but the doc's request-filter and create-side enums are INTEGERS and inconsistent with each other. Branch on the **response string**.
- `materialType` is mandatory on create but ABSENT from the response ‚Äî must be manufactured on push (default Finished Good=1).
- `accounting_unit` is the UOM signal but is dirty in real data ("111", "PCS") ‚Äî needs a default-UOM transform.
- `EANUPC` can be garbage ("testUomcode001") ‚Äî not used for matching (we match sku==item_code only).
- `cp_inventory` is explicitly ignorable. `product_id`/`cp_id` are internal (store for push, don't use as join key ‚Äî sku is the key).

## STAGE 1 ‚Äî The substrate: EasyEcom Item Map + mode flag

Build first, on its own, tested before any sync wiring:
- **`EasyEcom Item Map` DocType** (¬ß8.1.2): `ee_sku` (unique, the join key), a link to the ERPNext **Item OR Product Bundle** (Dynamic Link, or a `link_type` + `link_name` pair ‚Äî your call, but it MUST accommodate both object types), `ee_product_id`, `ee_cp_id` (stored for push), and a status field with values: Mapped, Created-Flagged, Flagged-Not-Created, Drift, Disabled. DB UNIQUE on `ee_sku`.
- **`item_master_mode`** setting (`onboarding` | `erpnext_mastered`) on the EasyEcom Account (or per-Company settings ‚Äî match where the other account-level config lives). Default `onboarding`. An explicit FDE action flips it (a button/field with a confirmation ‚Äî this is the "onboarding complete, ERPNext is now master" gate).
- Tests: map row CRUD, the dual-object link works for both an Item and a Product Bundle, UNIQUE on ee_sku enforced, mode flag defaults to onboarding and flips.

## STAGE 2 ‚Äî EE ‚Üí ERPNext pull (the inbound onboarding flow)

- **Reconcile the `EasyEcom-Item-Pull` engine ruleset against the real `GetProductMaster` payload FIRST** (the shipped ¬ß5 ruleset is stale ‚Äî written from an assumed payload; it has `item_code` where EE sends `sku`, etc. ‚Äî same stale-ruleset situation as Location-Pull and Channel-Pull). Run the real payload through Test Mapping; fix every path. Map: sku, product_name, description, brand, colour, size, dimensions (units: EE weight=g, dims=cm ‚Äî handle ERPNext's expected units), mrp, cost, hsn_code‚Üígst_hsn_code, accounting_unit‚Üístock_uom (**default-UOM transform** for dirty/missing), active‚Üídisabled (transform), custom_fields. Express derivations as ruleset transforms (¬ß8.0 ‚Äî engine = API-change insurance).
- **The pull job** (¬ß8.1.4): cursor-paginated, count-aware, **resumable background job** ‚Äî read GetProductMastersCount, walk `nextUrl` page-by-page (‚â§200/page), **savepoint-isolated per product** (reuse 8a `_isolation.py`), resume from last cursor if interrupted. NOT a single synchronous call (it would time out on ~15k products). `includeLocations=1` (account-wide). Full pull on onboarding; `updated_after` deltas on schedule.
- **Matching** (¬ß8.1.3): map row exists ‚Üí use it; else exact `sku == item_code` (byte-for-byte) ‚Üí auto-map + create map row; else ‚Üí create new Item + new map row. No fuzzy/EAN matching, no matching-review queue.
- **product_type branching** (on the response STRING): `normal_product` ‚Üí Item; `combo_product` ‚Üí Product Bundle (Stage 4 ‚Äî for now flag/skip or stub); `variant_parent`/`child_product`/`kit_bom`/**unknown** ‚Üí **flag Flagged-Not-Created, do NOT create.**
- **Tax**: call 8c's `resolve_and_stamp_tax(item, product)` per item.
- **Content gating** (¬ß8.1.4): a creatable product missing HSN / with unmapped tax / dirty UOM ‚Üí **create + flag Created-Flagged** (the SKU must exist for downstream orders; never hold, never silent-default).
- `active: 0` ‚Üí disable the item.
- Tests: cursor walk + resume; savepoint isolation (one bad product doesn't abort the page); count-aware paging; exact-match auto-map vs create-new; product_type branching incl. flag-not-created for variant/child/kit/unknown; content-gating create+flag; tax-resolver integration; active‚Üídisabled; ruleset reconciliation against the real payload fixture.

## STAGE 3 ‚Äî ERPNext ‚Üí EE push (outbound onboarding + the steady-state mechanism)

**This stage MUTATES EasyEcom (CreateMasterProduct actually creates products in EE). Smoke-test with a disposable test product and clean up ‚Äî unlike the read-only pull.**
- ERPNext item ‚Üí EE create-payload translation (¬ß8.1.5). **Manufacture EE-mandatory fields ERPNext lacks:** materialType ‚Üí default **Finished Good=1**; itemType (normal=0); Brand/Category/ModelNumber (source from item or default ‚Äî flag if a hard-mandatory field can't be sourced). ProductTaxCode = item HSN. TaxRate from item tax (EE allowed: 0/3/5/12/18/28). Dimensions in EE units.
- CreateMasterProduct (new) / UpdateMasterProduct (changed; keys sku or productId; partial) / on create, write returned `product_id` to the map row.
- Tests: create-payload field manufacturing (materialType default, itemType, etc.); product_id written back to map; update partial; a missing-mandatory-field case ‚Üí flagged not pushed. (Mutating sandbox tests: gated/marked so they don't run against prod; use a disposable SKU.)

## STAGE 4 ‚Äî Product Bundle ‚Üî EasyEcom combo (bidirectional)

- **Pull** (complete what Stage 2 stubbed): EE `combo_product` ‚Üí ERPNext **Product Bundle**; its `sub_products` ‚Üí bundle components, **each resolved to an ERPNext item via its own map row** (the map is the component-identity resolver).
- **Push**: ERPNext **Product Bundle** ‚Üí EE **combo** ("virtual combo" ‚Äî non-stocked wrapper; components carry stock). `CreateMasterProduct` with `itemType=1` + `subProducts` array, **each component resolved to its EE sku via its own map row**.
- **Dependency ordering**: components must exist in EE (and be mapped) before the combo is pushed ‚Äî process **components before combos**; missing/unmapped component at combo-push ‚Üí **flag, do NOT push** (no broken combos).
- **Constraint**: EE combo requires **‚â•2 subProducts**; a smaller bundle ‚Üí flag, can't push.
- The bundle gets its **own map row** (bundle ‚Üî combo); components resolve via their rows.
- **No BOM / no Kit**: ERPNext BOMs are NOT pushed; EE `kit_bom` is flagged-not-created on pull. Only Bundles ‚Üî combos cross.
- Tests: pull combo‚Üíbundle with component resolution; push bundle‚Üícombo with component resolution + dependency ordering; missing-component ‚Üí flag-not-push; <2-component ‚Üí flag; bundle map row + dual-object link; BOM never pushed.

## STAGE 5 ‚Äî Lifecycle + post-flip drift detection

- **Lifecycle** (¬ß8.1.7): onboarding ‚Äî EE active flag ‚Üí ERPNext disable/enable (pull), ERPNext disable ‚Üí EE ActivateDeactivateProduct (push). Steady state ‚Äî ERPNext disable/enable ‚Üí push to EE. Never delete.
- **The flip + drift** (¬ß8.1.8): after `item_master_mode = erpnext_mastered`, the push is authoritative; the pull becomes **drift detection only** ‚Äî an EE-origin new product or an EE-side edit to a mapped item is **flagged Drift for the FDE**, NOT accepted as truth and NOT auto-overwritten. The FDE decides per drift flag.
- **Supervised reconciliation** (¬ß8.1.9): during onboarding, duplicates/conflicts surfaced; FDE resolves; then flips.
- Tests: deactivate/reactivate both directions phase-governed; post-flip EE-origin product ‚Üí Drift flag (not created/accepted); post-flip EE edit to mapped item ‚Üí Drift flag (not overwritten); the flip changes pull behaviour from accept to drift-detect.

## Deferred / out of scope (do NOT build)
- **Marketplace listings** (Amazon ASIN, Flipkart FSN, per-channel marketplace_sku) ‚Äî separate per-channel listing sync (¬ß8.1.11).
- **Stock / inventory sync** (per-location quantities) ‚Äî separate concern from the product catalogue.
- No item duplication per Company (use ERPNext Item Defaults ‚Äî standard mechanism).

## Principles this packet must honor
- **¬ß8.0 engine policy** ‚Äî payload‚Üífield translation via the `EasyEcom-Item-Pull` ruleset (reconciled to real payload), never hardcoded. Flow code orchestrates (cursor job, branching, matching, tax call, gating, push).
- **¬ß2.7 no silent failure** ‚Äî every exception (content problem, unsupported type, drift, missing component, unpushable) is a VISIBLE flagged state, never a silent default or silent overwrite.
- **¬ß7.1 isolation** ‚Äî the pull batch reuses the 8a savepoint helper; one product's failure never aborts the page/walk.
- **Frappe-native** ‚Äî ERPNext Item, Product Bundle, Item Defaults, standard Workflow; the map is a standard DocType.
- **Identity is never auto-guessed** ‚Äî match only on exact sku==item_code; everything else creates new (a visible duplicate) rather than risk a wrong link (silent corruption). Remap/divergence is FDE-resolved, never automatic.

---

# Build instruction

Build in the five stages above, **in order, each green before the next**. Commit locally at each stage boundary (the user reviews/pushes; do NOT push). 

1. **Inventory first**: confirm ERPNext Item + Product Bundle DocTypes and their fields; the `EasyEcom-Item-Pull` ruleset's current (stale) state; 8c's `resolve_and_stamp_tax` signature; the 8a `_isolation.py` helper; the workflow-fixture pattern. Report drift; if anything contradicts ¬ß8.1, STOP and report.
2. **Stage 1** (map + mode substrate) ‚Üí test ‚Üí commit.
3. **Stage 2** (pull: reconcile ruleset first, then the cursor job, matching, branching, tax, gating) ‚Üí test ‚Üí commit. This is the big one; the pull is read-only toward EE so it's safe to exercise against the sandbox.
4. **Stage 3** (push) ‚Üí test ‚Üí commit. MUTATING ‚Äî use a disposable test SKU, clean up, gate mutating tests.
5. **Stage 4** (bundle ‚Üî combo both ways, component resolution, dependency ordering) ‚Üí test ‚Üí commit.
6. **Stage 5** (lifecycle + flip + drift) ‚Üí test ‚Üí commit.

**Done when:** bench migrate clean; the map links items/bundles both ways; the pull ingests the real catalogue (cursor-walked, resumable, savepoint-isolated) with correct branching/matching/tax/gating; the push creates EE products/combos with manufactured mandatory fields and writes back product_id; bundles round-trip as virtual combos with component resolution + dependency ordering; lifecycle works both ways; the flip switches the pull to drift-detection; suite green.

**Report at each stage**: what you verified vs built, the ruleset reconciliation (Stage 2), the cursor/resume + savepoint behaviour, the matching outcomes, the push field-manufacturing + product_id writeback, the bundle component-resolution + dependency ordering, and the flip/drift behaviour. For the mutating push smoke (Stage 3), report exactly what was created in the sandbox and that it was cleaned up.

---

# Spec body ‚Äî ¬ß8.1 Item / Product master (authoritative)

## 8.1 Item / Product master (bidirectional onboarding ‚Üí ERPNext-mastered steady state)

Item sync is the largest master. It is **bidirectional during onboarding** and **ERPNext-mastered in steady state**, with an explicit flip between the two phases. The design is grounded in the real EasyEcom Product Master API (five functions: GetProductMaster, GetProductMastersCount, CreateMasterProduct, UpdateMasterProduct, ActivateDeactivateProduct) and real `GetProductMaster` payloads.

### 8.1.1 Two phases, one explicit flip

- **Onboarding (bidirectional, supervised, one-time per client):** products may already exist on either side. The integration pulls EasyEcom products into ERPNext **and** pushes ERPNext items into EasyEcom, reconciling both catalogues into a single mapped set. This is a supervised migration, not a steady state ‚Äî duplicates and conflicts are surfaced for FDE resolution.
- **Steady state (ERPNext-mastered):** once the FDE marks onboarding complete, **ERPNext is the source of truth for items.** New items and changes flow ERPNext ‚Üí EasyEcom (push). EasyEcom is downstream for the item master.
- **The flip is explicit:** a mode flag (`item_master_mode`: `onboarding` | `erpnext_mastered`) on the EasyEcom Account / per-client settings. The FDE flips it only after reconciliation is complete. Behaviour differs by phase (below).

### 8.1.2 The EasyEcom Item Map (direction-agnostic correspondence registry)

The link between an EasyEcom product and an ERPNext item/bundle is an explicit, persistent record ‚Äî **not** a field-equality computation. This is the single source of linkage for both directions and all downstream flows (orders, inventory).

- `EasyEcom Item Map` ‚Äî one row per correspondence:
  - `ee_sku` (the EasyEcom SKU ‚Äî EE's unique product identifier per its own FAQ; the join key)
  - `erpnext_item` (the linked ERPNext Item **or** Product Bundle ‚Äî the link accommodates both object types, via a Dynamic Link or a type field)
  - `ee_product_id`, `ee_cp_id` (EasyEcom internal ids ‚Äî stored because the push functions, Update/ActivateDeactivate, key on `product_id`; not our join key)
  - status / flags (Mapped, Created-Flagged, Flagged-Not-Created, Drift, Disabled)
- The map is **direction-agnostic**: a row is created whether the product was born in EasyEcom (pull created it) or in ERPNext (push created it in EE). It is also the **component-identity resolver** for combos/bundles in both directions (below).

### 8.1.3 Matching (deliberately simple)

EasyEcom guarantees SKU uniqueness (its FAQ: "SKU is the most unique identifier"; "sellers are mandated to maintain unique SKU codes"). So:

- **Map row already exists** ‚Üí use it.
- **EE `sku` exactly equals an ERPNext `item_code`** (byte-for-byte, case- and whitespace-sensitive) ‚Üí **auto-map** to that item + create the map row.
- **Anything else** ‚Üí **create a new ERPNext item + a new map row.**

There is no fuzzy matching, no EAN-based matching, and no matching-review queue. The chosen error direction is "never wrongly link" over "never duplicate": a non-exact case creates a new item (a visible, fixable duplicate at worst) rather than risk an incorrect auto-link (silent data corruption). `item_code` defaults to `sku` for items created by the pull.

### 8.1.4 EE ‚Üí ERPNext (pull)

The pull is the **inbound onboarding** flow, and after the flip becomes **drift detection** (below).

- **Endpoint:** `GET /Products/GetProductMaster` (headers `x-api-key` + per-account `Bearer <jwt>`). `includeLocations=1` ‚Üí **account-wide catalogue** (products are a catalogue keyed by globally-unique SKU; not a per-location sweep). `GET /Products/GetProductMastersCount` gives the total for progress.
- **Job shape:** the catalogue is large (counts in the tens of thousands). The pull is a **cursor-paginated, count-aware, resumable background job**: read the count, then walk the `nextUrl` cursor page by page (‚â§200/page), **savepoint-isolated per product** (one bad product does not abort the page or the walk; the job resumes from the last cursor if interrupted). It is **not** a single synchronous call (which would time out).
- **Incremental:** full pull on onboarding; `updated_after` deltas on the daily schedule.
- **Field translation via the Field Mapping engine** (the `EasyEcom-Item-Pull` ruleset, reconciled to the real `GetProductMaster` payload ‚Äî the shipped ruleset is stale, ¬ß8.0 policy). Maps `sku`, `product_name`, `description`, `brand`, `colour`, `size`, dimensions (with unit handling), `mrp`, `cost`, `hsn_code` ‚Üí `gst_hsn_code`, `accounting_unit` ‚Üí `stock_uom` (with a **default-UOM** transform for missing/garbage values ‚Äî EE supplies no clean stock UOM), `active` ‚Üí `disabled`, `custom_fields`.
- **Tax:** calls **8c's `resolve_and_stamp_tax(item, product)`** per item (the product carries `tax_rule_name`, `tax_rate`, `cess`). Unmapped tax rule ‚Üí 8c auto-creates a To-Configure Tax Rule Map + flags FDE.
- **product_type branching** (on the **response string** value; the doc's integer enums are inconsistent across endpoints and are a push-side concern only):
  - `normal_product` ‚Üí ERPNext **Item**
  - `combo_product` ‚Üí ERPNext **Product Bundle** (its `sub_products` become bundle components, each resolved to an ERPNext item via its own map row)
  - `variant_parent` / `child_product` / `kit_bom` / **any unknown or future type (e.g. a future "digital" type)** ‚Üí **flag, do NOT create.** Variants, variant children, and kits/BOMs should not exist in an EasyEcom‚ÜîERPNext item integration (manufacturing/variant modelling stays ERPNext-side); their appearance in the EE pull is an exception for the FDE, not a creation target. Unknown types fall through to the same flag (forward-safe).
- **Content gating (create + flag):** a creatable product (normal/combo) that is missing HSN, has an unmapped tax rule, or has a dirty UOM is **created and flagged** (so the SKU exists for downstream orders) and surfaced in the review queue ‚Äî never silently defaulted, never held.
- `active: 0` in the payload ‚Üí the ERPNext item is **disabled** (never deleted).

### 8.1.5 ERPNext ‚Üí EE (push)

The push is the **outbound onboarding** flow **and** the **steady-state mechanism** (once ERPNext is master, all item flow is push).

- **Endpoints:** `POST /Products/CreateMasterProduct` (new), `POST /Products/UpdateMasterProduct` (changed; keys on sku or productId; supports partial updates), `POST /Products/ActivateDeactivateProduct` (lifecycle; keys on product_id).
- **Field translation** ERPNext item ‚Üí EE create-payload. EasyEcom's create requires fields ERPNext items do not natively carry ‚Äî these are **manufactured/defaulted** on push: `materialType` (mandatory in EE; absent in ERPNext ‚Üí default **Finished Good = 1**), `itemType` (from the product's nature: normal = 0, combo = 1), `Brand` / `Category` / `ModelNumber` (sourced from the item or defaulted). `ProductTaxCode` = the item's HSN. `TaxRate` from the item's tax (allowed EE values 0/3/5/12/18/28). Dimensions in EE units (weight g, dimensions cm).
- On `CreateMasterProduct`, EE returns `product_id` ‚Üí written to the map row.

### 8.1.6 Product Bundles ‚Üî EasyEcom combos (bidirectional)

Bundles are bidirectional, mirroring normal items:

- **Pull:** EE `combo_product` ‚Üí ERPNext Product Bundle (covered in 8.1.4).
- **Push:** an ERPNext **Product Bundle** ‚Üí an EasyEcom **combo** (a "virtual combo" ‚Äî non-stocked selling wrapper; the components carry the stock, exactly as a Product Bundle is non-stocked in ERPNext). Pushed via `CreateMasterProduct` with `itemType = 1` (Combo) and the `subProducts` array assembled from the bundle's components ‚Äî **each component resolved to its EasyEcom SKU via its own map row.**
- **Dependency ordering:** components must exist in EasyEcom (and be mapped) before the combo is pushed. The push processes **components before combos**; if a component is missing or unmapped at combo-push time, the combo is **flagged, not pushed** (no broken combos).
- **Constraint:** EasyEcom requires a combo's `subProducts` to contain **at least 2 products**; a bundle with fewer cannot be pushed and is flagged.
- **The bundle gets its own map row** (bundle ‚Üî EE combo); its components resolve through their own rows. The map is thus the **component-identity resolver in both directions** (sub_product sku ‚Üí ERPNext item on pull; ERPNext component ‚Üí EE sku on push).
- **No BOM / no Kit:** ERPNext BOMs are **not** pushed to EasyEcom (manufacturing stays ERPNext-side); EasyEcom `kit_bom` is flagged-not-created on pull. Only Product Bundles ‚Üî combos cross the boundary.

### 8.1.7 Lifecycle ‚Äî deactivate / reactivate (both directions, phase-governed)

- **Onboarding:** EE `active` flag ‚Üí ERPNext disable/enable (pull side); ERPNext disable ‚Üí EE `ActivateDeactivateProduct(status=0)` (push side).
- **Steady state (ERPNext-mastered):** ERPNext disable/enable ‚Üí pushed to EE. An EE-side deactivation is **not** accepted as truth ‚Äî it is **flagged as drift** (below).
- Deactivation never deletes on either side.

### 8.1.8 After the flip ‚Äî drift detection

Once `item_master_mode = erpnext_mastered`:

- The push (ERPNext ‚Üí EE) is the authoritative item flow.
- The pull continues only as **drift detection**: an EE-originated new product, or an EE-side edit to a mapped item, is **flagged for the FDE** ‚Äî not accepted as truth, and **not auto-overwritten** from ERPNext (silently overwriting could destroy legitimate EE operational data; silently accepting would violate ERPNext mastership). The FDE decides per drift flag.

### 8.1.9 Onboarding reconciliation (supervised) and the exception/review state

- Onboarding reconciliation is **FDE-supervised**: after pulling EE products and pushing ERPNext items, duplicates (same physical product reached from both sides with non-matching identifiers) and conflicts are surfaced; the FDE resolves them; then the FDE flips to `erpnext_mastered`.
- The **review/exception state** holds three kinds of items, all visible to the FDE:
  - **Created-Flagged** ‚Äî created but with a content problem (missing HSN, unmapped tax, dirty UOM) to fix.
  - **Flagged-Not-Created** ‚Äî an unsupported type (variant_parent / child_product / kit_bom / unknown) that was not created.
  - **Drift** ‚Äî (post-flip) an unexpected EE-side change or EE-origin product.

### 8.1.10 Build sequence (one packet, internally sequential)

Built as a single packet, in dependency order: (1) the `EasyEcom Item Map` + the `item_master_mode` substrate; (2) the EE ‚Üí ERPNext pull (cursor job, engine ruleset reconciliation, tax-resolver integration, product_type branching, content gating); (3) the ERPNext ‚Üí EE push (create/update, EE-mandatory-field manufacturing); (4) Product Bundle ‚Üî combo both ways (component resolution, dependency ordering); (5) lifecycle (deactivate/reactivate, phase-governed) and the post-flip drift detection. Reuses the Field Mapping engine (¬ß8.0), 8c's tax resolver, the 8a per-record savepoint helper, and the workflow-fixture pattern (and its captured gotchas, including the `safe_eval`/`len()` condition rule).

### 8.1.11 Out of scope (separate, later)

- **Marketplace listings** (Amazon ASIN, Flipkart FSN, per-channel `marketplace_sku`): synced per-channel from the marketplaces into EasyEcom (EE FAQ); a separate listing-sync concern, not part of the item master.
- **Stock / inventory sync** (per-location quantities): a separate concern from the product master (which is the account-wide catalogue).
- Account-global sharing across Companies via ERPNext Item Defaults is the standard ERPNext mechanism; the integration does not duplicate the Item per Company.
